rulings.Case Title: HEATHER SIBLEY ET AL VS. JACQUELINE KING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Confirming Contractual Arbitration Award; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2019 on Line 1, PETITIONER HEATHER SIBLEY, BRIAN WISNIEWSKI Motion For Order Confirming Contractual Arbitration Award; Points And Authorities Grant. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court will also sign the proposed judgment included with the moving papers. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. AYA CUZNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2019, line 1. PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION TO DEEM REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff's Requests for Admissions, Set One, are deemed admitted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MUSONDA KABWE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2019 on Line 3, DEFENDANT MUSONDA KABWE MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Off calendar. Defendant did not serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code Civ. Proc., § 1005(b). In view of the strong policy favoring trial of cases on their merits, and Defendant's prompt action in moving to set aside the original default, the Court urges Plaintiff to stipulate to set aside the default to avoid additional unnecessary motion practice. (See Elston v. City of Turlock (1985) 38 Cal.3d 227, 233 [where the party in default moves promptly to seek relief, and no prejudice to the opposing party will result from setting aside the default and letting the case go to trial on the merits, "very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside the default."]; see generally Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127.) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JACKSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC WORKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2019, line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED. Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's combined motion is granted in part and denied in part. Within five (5) court days after service of entry of this order, Plaintiff shall serve upon Defendant, code-compliant responses to: 1) Defendant's Form Interrogatories-General, Set One, to Plaintiff; 2) Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set One, to Plaintiff; and 3) Defendant's Request for Production of Documents, Set One, to Plaintiff. Defendant's Requests for Admissions, Set One, to Plaintiff did not comply with C.C.P. § 2033.060(e). Thus, the motion to deem admissions admitted is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to wlynn@rroyselaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN RUBIN VS. CHRISTINA LEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, December 27, 2019 on Line 2, PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN RUBIN Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion for an award of sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,310. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
